target: ISS-677: PROS detail_reasoning + Part 6 prospective tracker prose (Section 6.11)

pros_detail_reasoning.description: detail_reasoning text for each PROS entry in predictions.json. These render as analysis panels on the Predictions Analysis tab.

entries.detail_reasoning: Promoted to WIN-004 (SAA exponential cell separation). The SAA splitting into African and South Atlantic cells is well-documented by Terra-Nova et al. (2017) and NOAA EMM data, with the separation trend visible in IGRF coefficients since at least the early 2000s. The dome registration date (2026-01-01) is years after the phenomenon was published. The dome provides no fluid-dynamic mechanism connecting its flat-disc geometry to reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary — the established cause of SAA spatial structure. Our WIN-004 verdict (Std Model Explains) applies: MHD core dynamics derive the splitting from first principles; the dome offers only a label. By the author's own framework, this is backtested — data predates registration.

Promoted to WIN-007 (NMP post-1990 acceleration). The NMP acceleration from ~15 km/yr to ~60 km/yr around 1990 is extensively documented in geomagnetic literature (Chulliat & Newitt 2010, Livermore et al. 2020). NOAA NP.xy datasets published this trend before the dome's registration date. The dome claims the acceleration follows its aetheric decay model but provides no derivation connecting disc geometry to core flux lobe dynamics — the established mechanism. Our WIN-007 verdict (Misleading) applies: standard geophysics already explains the acceleration; the dome provides a label with no predictive content. Data predates registration by decades.

SUSPENDED — the dome's only genuinely prospective prediction with a closed test window. PROS-003 predicted that eclipse magnetic anomalies would track eclipse geometry (umbral shadow position). The eclipse timing audit showed Z-component minima led or lagged the umbra by 34–104 minutes across stations — inconsistent with geometric tracking. The March 2024 eclipse was further contaminated by a Kp 5–6 geomagnetic storm. The dome silently changed PROS-003's status to "suspended" in the 2026-03-15 batch alongside WIN-010/WIN-025 suspensions, with no public acknowledgment of falsification. This matches the pattern documented in Section 6.5: failed predictions are quietly removed rather than scored as failures. PROS-003 is now tracked as FAIL-008 in our uncounted-failures tally. This is the most informative PROS entry: the one genuinely novel prediction with a closed test window was falsified.

Promoted to WIN-024 (Roaring 40s = SAA southern boundary). The Roaring 40s wind belt (40–50°S) is driven by Coriolis forces and atmospheric pressure gradients from Ferrel cell circulation — a first-principles prediction of standard atmospheric physics unrelated to geomagnetic field structure. The latitude coincidence between the wind belt and the SAA's southern extent is not a prediction but a geographic correlation. The dome provides no physical mechanism linking aetheric currents to atmospheric circulation patterns. Our WIN-024 verdict (Misleading) applies: latitude coincidence presented as causal connection. Data predates registration.

Promoted to WIN-035 (SAA African cell intensity < 21,795 nT). The SAA African cell has been decaying at ~28–30 nT/yr for decades, directly measurable from INTERMAGNET station data and published in WMM2025 forecasts (released December 2024). Setting a threshold of 21,795 nT on a monotonically declining trend guarantees eventual confirmation — the prediction is that a falling quantity will fall below a level it was already approaching. Our WIN-035 verdict (Std Model Explains) applies: the dome extrapolates the same station trends as WMM2025 with no unique dome-derived prediction. Data predates registration.

Promoted to WIN-036 (NMP deviation > 18° from 120°E). WMM2025 (published December 2024, over 14 months before PROS-006 registration) placed the NMP at ~139.3°E — already deviating more than 19° from 120°E. The dome registered the prediction after the data was publicly available and the threshold already exceeded. Our WIN-036 verdict (Std Model Explains) applies: the dome extrapolated NOAA's published trajectory. This is retrodiction presented as prediction, meeting the author's own definition of "backtested."

Promoted to WIN-037 (field decay ≥28 nT since March 2025). This is one of only two genuinely prospective PROS items (registered before the test window closed). However, the 28 nT threshold is set within the established ~28–30 nT/yr SAA decay trend — a rate both the dome and WMM2025 agree on. The dome's confirmation text admits the measurement is regional (SAA/Tsumeb station), not global as the prediction claims. Our WIN-037 verdict (Misleading) applies: the threshold was set within an established trend, guaranteeing confirmation; the metric is a model input parameter (28 nT/yr baseline), not a derived output. Standard physics predicts the same decay rate.

Promoted to WIN-038 (Schumann resonance persists at 7.83 Hz). The 7.83 Hz fundamental Schumann resonance has been continuously observed since Balser & Wagner's 1962 measurements and is a standard prediction of any Earth-ionosphere cavity model (Schumann 1952). The dome's own cavity geometry (H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) km) predicts a fundamental frequency of ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz — making this prediction a self-contradiction rather than a confirmation. Our WIN-038 verdict (Self-Contradicted) applies: the dome predicts the wrong frequency but claims the observed frequency as a success. Data predates registration by 60+ years.

section_prose.description: New Section 6.11 for Part 6 (sections.json part6). Analyzes the dome's prospective prediction bucket as a whole.
section_prose.title: 6.11 The Prospective Bucket — Testing the Model's Own Predictive Standard
section_prose.html:
6.11 The Prospective Bucket — Testing the Model's Own Predictive Standard

The dome maintains a distinct "prospective" bucket — predictions registered before confirmation — alongside its confirmed WINs. This separation deserves credit: the author explicitly distinguishes backtested claims from prospective ones and states that "anyone can fit a model to old data." By the author's own standard, the prospective bucket is where the model's genuine predictive power should be measured. So what does it show?

Of the {{PROS_TOTAL}} prospective items cataloged, {{PROS_PROMOTED}} were promoted to confirmed WINs and {{PROS_SUSPENDED}} was silently suspended. On the surface, a 7-of-8 promotion rate looks impressive. But examining each promoted item reveals a consistent pattern: every promotion adopts data that was publicly available before registration, uses thresholds set within established trends, and matches phenomena that standard geophysics already explains from first principles. Our independent assessment finds that zero of the eight prospective items survive as genuine dome-derived predictions — each is either standard physics relabeled (PROS-001, 002, 004, 005, 006, 007, 008) or falsified (PROS-003).

The most informative entry is PROS-003 (eclipse magnetic anomaly tracks geometry) — the only genuinely prospective item with a closed test window. The eclipse timing audit showed Z-component minima leading or lagging the umbra by 34–104 minutes across stations, inconsistent with the geometric tracking the prediction required. Rather than acknowledging the falsification, the dome silently changed PROS-003's status to "suspended" alongside two WIN suspensions (2026-03-15 batch), with no public explanation. This matches the pattern documented in Section 6.5: failed predictions are quietly removed rather than scored as failures, and the accuracy denominator is adjusted to exclude them.

The prospective bucket thus confirms, rather than contradicts, the structural patterns identified throughout this review. The author understands that prospective predictions carry more evidential weight than retrodictions — and has built infrastructure to track them. But the bucket's contents reveal that even the dome's self-identified strongest predictions reduce to standard physics, retrodiction of known trends, or silent suspension when the data disagrees. By the author's own predictive standard, the model's prospective track record is 0-for-1 on genuinely novel predictions with closed test windows.
section_prose.build_changes_needed: generate-html.js needs to compute PROS_TOTAL, PROS_PROMOTED, PROS_SUSPENDED from predictions.json and inject into section placeholders. These are simple count queries.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's prospective bucket is more rigorous than most alternative-science models. The author genuinely separates backtested from prospective claims, which is an epistemological concession that strengthens our review: we are testing the model by a standard the author himself endorses. Our section must acknowledge this credit before showing that the bucket's contents do not survive scrutiny.

other_side.dome_defense: A dome defender could argue: (a) 7/8 promoted means the model works; (b) PROS-003 was suspended due to contaminating data (Kp 5-6 storm), not model failure; (c) backtested vs prospective is a distinction about timing, not about correctness. Defense (b) has partial merit — geomagnetic storms DO contaminate eclipse magnetic measurements. But the suspension was silent, and the dome's monitor.py already includes a Kp<2 escape clause that should have filtered contaminated events BEFORE PROS-003 was registered.
other_side.our_response: The strongest counter to the dome defense is the author's own framework. By his standard, backtested claims (6 of 8 PROS items) carry "lower evidential weight" because "anyone can fit a model to old data." We are simply applying his criteria. On PROS-003: if the Kp escape clause was known before registration, why wasn't it built into the prediction? Either the escape clause was added post-hoc (moving the goalposts) or it was known and PROS-003 was registered anyway (accepting the contamination risk). Neither interpretation supports the dome.
